question: Use hsb-ip-create-top to create an FPGA_top.sv scaffold from my validated HOLOLINK_def.svh. Use the bundled known-rev port reference if no HSB IP source checkout is obvious.
ground_truth: The agent reads the hsb-ip-create-top SKILL.md, recognizes this as Standalone Create Top, requires a validated HOLOLINK_def.svh, uses the bundled known-rev reference when live HOLOLINK_top.sv source is not available, loads the fixed-format guidance, preserves HOLOLINK_top signal shapes, and produces or plans a fixed-format FPGA_top.sv scaffold.
expected_behavior: The agent reads hsb-ip-create-top/SKILL.md before taking action
The agent recognizes the request as Standalone Create Top
The agent requires a validated HOLOLINK_def.svh before generating FPGA_top.sv
The agent uses references/source-policy.md and references/fixed-format.md when live HSB IP source is unavailable
The agent preserves HOLOLINK_top port shapes, using unpacked arrays for multi-interface vector buses such as *_tdata, *_tkeep, and *_tuser
The agent declares APB with scalar paddr, pwdata, penable, and pwrite; vector psel, pready, and pserr; and unpacked apb_prdata[REG_INST-1:0]
The agent does not generate or validate HOLOLINK_def.svh itself
The agent summarizes emitted HSB signal groups and uses the generic reference/template note

question: Create a top-level FPGA_top.sv wrapper for HSB, but I do not have a HOLOLINK_def.svh yet.
ground_truth: The agent reads the hsb-ip-create-top SKILL.md, stops top-generation work because no validated HOLOLINK_def.svh exists, and directs the user to hsb-ip-def for HOLOLINK_def.svh generation and validation before resuming top creation.
expected_behavior: The agent reads hsb-ip-create-top/SKILL.md before taking action
The agent does not create FPGA_top.sv without a validated HOLOLINK_def.svh
The agent directs the user to hsb-ip-def for defs generation and validation
The agent asks for the validated defs file path or content before proceeding
The agent avoids inventing macro settings or project-specific integration details

question: Why does the generated FPGA_top.sv include different HSB signal groups depending on my HOLOLINK_def.svh macros?
ground_truth: The agent reads the hsb-ip-create-top SKILL.md, recognizes this as an Explain workflow, explains that active signal groups are derived from validated defs macros and the HOLOLINK_top port map, prefers live HOLOLINK_top.sv when available, and otherwise relies on the bundled known-rev reference.
expected_behavior: The agent reads hsb-ip-create-top/SKILL.md before answering
The agent recognizes the request as an Explain workflow
The agent explains that defs macros gate which HSB-facing signal groups appear
The agent prefers live HOLOLINK_top.sv as the source of truth when available
The agent uses bundled references only when live source is unavailable
The agent defers detailed HOLOLINK_def.svh macro meaning to hsb-ip-def
